[[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:関金温泉]] {{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | External Links = [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}}] | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.
== References == {{Footnote Help}} <references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}}] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}}]

== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.

[[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:関金温泉]] {{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | External Links = [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.

[[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:関金温泉]] {{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.

In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.

[[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:関金温泉]] {{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.

[[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:関金温泉]] {{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive springs|lt=Radon-containing}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.

In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.
== References == {{Footnote Help}} <references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]]

[[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:関金温泉]] {{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.
== References == {{Footnote Help}} <references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]]

[[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:三朝温泉]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}}[[Category:11]] <!--Misasa Onsen--> [[ja:関金温泉]] {{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.
== References == {{Footnote Help}} <references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]]
{{Hot Spring}}

Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|12=draft}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|12=draft}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|12=draft}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|12=draft}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|12=draft}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|12=draft}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|12=draft}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|12=draft}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.
== References == {{Footnote Help}} <references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂|12=draft}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]]

{{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|12=draft}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|12=draft}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景|12=draft}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉|12=draft}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷|12=draft}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|12=draft}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|12=draft}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|12=draft}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉|12=draft}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|12=draft}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|12=draft}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|12=draft}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|12=draft}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|12=draft}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|12=draft}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|12=draft}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|12=draft}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.
== References == {{Footnote Help}} <references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂|12=draft}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]]

{{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|12=draft}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|12=draft}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景|12=draft}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉|12=draft}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷|12=draft}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|12=draft}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|12=draft}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|12=draft}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉|12=draft}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|12=draft}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|12=draft}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|12=draft}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|12=draft}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|12=draft}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|12=draft}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|12=draft}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|12=draft}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.

== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|12=draft}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|12=draft}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|12=draft}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|12=draft}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|12=draft}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|12=draft}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|12=draft}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|12=draft}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.
