{{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|12=draft}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|12=draft}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景|12=draft}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉|12=draft}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷|12=draft}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|12=draft}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|12=draft}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|12=draft}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉|12=draft}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|12=draft}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|12=draft}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|12=draft}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|12=draft}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|12=draft}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|12=draft}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|12=draft}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|12=draft}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.

{{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|12=draft}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|12=draft}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=draft}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|12=draft}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|12=draft}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景|12=draft}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉|12=draft}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷|12=draft}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|12=draft}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|12=draft}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|12=draft}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉|12=draft}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|12=draft}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|12=draft}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|12=draft}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|12=draft}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|12=draft}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|12=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|12=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|12=draft}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|12=draft}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|12=draft}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|12=draft}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|12=draft}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|12=draft}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|12=draft}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|12=draft}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|12=draft}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|12=draft}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|12=draft}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21.
== References == {{Footnote Help}} <references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂|12=draft}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]] {{citation needed|Misasa Onsen}} <!--Misasa Onsen-->

{{coord|35|24|38.7|N|133|53|35.1|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 三朝温泉 |画像 = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg |コメント = 河原風呂と三徳川 |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[東伯郡]][[三朝町]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道：JR[[山陰本線]][[倉吉駅]]より、[[日ノ丸自動車|日ノ丸バス]]で20 - 25分 |泉質 = [[塩化物泉]]、[[単純温泉]]、[[放射能泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=三朝温泉公式サイト |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher=鳥取県 輝く鳥取創造本部 観光交流局 観光戦略課 |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> |統計年度 = 2023 |外部リンク = [https://misasaonsen.jp/ 三朝温泉公式サイト] |特記事項 = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = 三朝温泉の位置 | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = 三朝温泉の位置 | alt = 三朝温泉の位置 }}
'''三朝温泉'''（みささ おんせん）は、[[鳥取県]][[東伯郡]][[三朝町]]にある[[温泉]]。[[日本百景]]。
2017年現在、年間入湯客数は鳥取県内では[[皆生温泉]]に次ぐ2位<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023" />。とっとり梨の花温泉郷を構成する温泉のひとつ。
== 泉質 == * 含放射能/ナトリウム・[[塩化物泉]] * 含放射能/単純泉
[[三徳川]]によって南北に三朝群（三朝区）と山田群（山田区）の二つの温泉群に分けられる<ref name="morinaga" /><ref name="horiuchi">[http://www.j-hss.org/journal/back_number/vol64_pdf/vol64no4_409_421.pdf 堀内公子、箕輪はるか、吉澤幸夫「三朝温泉におけるラドン研究の 100 年」] 温泉科学64巻、2023年2月27日閲覧。</ref>。日本最大規模の[[ラドン]]温泉地域で、泉温摂氏34度以上のラドン温泉は世界的にも希少な[[放射能泉]]である<ref name="horiuchi" />。
温泉療法を実施する医療機関があり、温泉は泉質に応じて温泉プール療法、[[飲泉]]療法、鉱泥療法など様々な治療に用いられている<ref>[http://www.okayama-u.ac.jp/user/misasa/spa/index.html 温泉療法 - 岡山大学病院三朝医療センター]（2012年7月21日閲覧）</ref><ref>[http://www.hosp.misasa.tottori.jp/about/onsen.html 当院の温泉について - 社団法人鳥取県中部医師会立三朝温泉病院]（2012年7月21日閲覧）</ref>以上により、高い[[ホルミシス効果]]があるとされ、[[観光客]]だけでなく療養目的で訪れる[[湯治]]客も多い<ref>{{Cite web |title=三朝温泉の効能と歴史 {{!}} 三朝温泉ポータルサイト│宿泊施設＆観光情報満載【三朝温泉 公式サイト】 |url=https://misasaonsen.jp/about/ |website=misasaonsen.jp |date=2020-03-20 |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=ラドン熱気浴 {{!}} 健康づくりの湯治宿 ブランナールみささ |url=https://www.blancart.jp/spa |work=健康づくりの湯治宿 ブランナールみささ |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref>。
== 温泉街 == 三徳川の両岸に[[旅館]]が立ち並ぶ<ref name="horiuchi" />。また、三朝大橋のたもとに河原風呂（川原の温泉：24時間（奇数日午前中は清掃）・無料）がある<ref>[https://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 三朝温泉河原風呂] 公益社団法人 鳥取県観光連盟、2023年3月15日閲覧。</ref>。[[石畳]]が敷かれた温泉本通りには、こぢんまりした旅館や飲食店、古美術店、スナック、[[土産物]]屋、[[射的]]場等が並んでおり、情緒ある[[温泉街]]が形成されている。三朝温泉最古の源泉とされる「株湯」は温泉街から少し離れた静かな住宅街の中にあり、地元住民の利用が多い<ref name="horiuchi" />。
本格的な療養温泉でもあり、[[温泉療法]]を実施する病院や研究施設がある。また温水床暖房を持った長期滞在者向けの旅館や自炊宿も見られ、[[観光]]と療養（[[湯治]]）の並立が三朝温泉の特徴である<ref name="horiuchi" />。二十数軒ある旅館・[[ホテル]]の多くが、「現代湯治」に対応している。温泉街にある病院での診察と組み合わせたり、[[ソムリエ]]をもじった入浴アドバイザー「ラヂムリエ」がいたりする旅館もある<ref>[http://spa-misasa.jp/toji/#toji_lodging 三朝温泉の現代湯治]（2018年4月30日閲覧）</ref>。
=== 温泉施設 === * [[外湯]] - 河原風呂、株湯 * [[足湯]] - 河原の湯、薬師の湯、かじかの湯、株湯 * [[飲泉]]場<ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=https://misasaonsen.jp/sightseeing/onsen | title=三朝温泉 温泉施設 | website=三朝温泉公式サイト | publisher=三朝温泉観光協会／三朝温泉旅館協同組合 | accessdate=2021年11月10日}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=https://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/176 | title=三朝温泉 | website=鳥取県観光案内 とっとり旅の生情報 | publisher=公益社団法人 鳥取県観光連盟 | accessdate=2021年11月10日}}</ref> - 薬師の湯、株湯、三朝神社（神の湯）
なお、三朝町営の[[国民宿舎]]として'''ブランナールみささ'''が設置された<ref>[http://www2.town.misasa.tottori.jp/reiki_int/reiki_honbun/m023RG00000378.html 三朝町国民宿舎事業の設置等に関する条例]</ref>。旧名称は三朝温泉会館で、[[1963年]]（昭和38年）にオープンし、[[1995年]]（平成7年）にブランナールみささに改名した。国民宿舎ブランナールみささは、[[2023年]]（令和5年）に指定管理者ジーライオンのグループ会社に譲渡されることになった<ref>[https://www.nnn.co.jp/articles/-/18978 三朝町「ブランナール」民間譲渡 指定管理者グループ会社に] 日本海新聞、2023年2月27日閲覧。</ref>。
また、温泉街には[[公衆浴場]]「たまわりの湯」が設けられ、住民や観光客に利用されてきたが、施設の老朽化と従業員の高齢化により、2023年3月末で営業を終了することになった<ref name="nnn20230314">[https://www.nnn.co.jp/articles/-/34336 ”癒やしの浴場”３月末閉館 三朝温泉・たまわりの湯 町が新施設計画も] 日本海新聞、2023年3月15日閲覧。</ref>。この共同浴場「たまわりの湯」の跡地についても、2024年（令和6年）5月に高級旅館の建設を計画するジーライオングループに売却する方針が発表された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nnn.co.jp/articles/-/318771 |title=たまわりの湯跡地 売却へ 神戸の企業に 地元おおむね了承 三朝町|accessdate=2024-05-23 |publisher=日本海新聞 |language=ja}}</ref>。
=== 周辺施設 === * [[病院]] - 三朝温泉病院（2015年末で閉鎖の[[岡山大学病院三朝医療センター]]の機能も継承<ref>[https://mainichi.jp/articles/20151225/ddl/k31/040/506000c 三朝医療センター：岡大が今月末閉院 温泉病院が機能継承]『毎日新聞』朝刊2015年12月25日（鳥取県版）2018年4月30日閲覧</ref>） <!--療養型と思われるもの--> * 研究所 - [[岡山大学惑星物質研究所]]（岡山大学所管の共同利用・共同研究拠点） * [[博物館]]・[[美術館]] - [[三朝バイオリン美術館]]、[[陣所の館]] 、湯の街ギャラリー * その他 - [[キュリー広場]]、[[マリ・キュリー|キュリー夫人]]の胸像、[[木屋旅館]]（2010年3月 建物が国の[[登録有形文化財]]に登録）、[[旅館大橋]]（1932年築の国の[[登録有形文化財]]の旅館）
== 歴史 == 伝説によれば、[[平安時代]]末期の[[1164年]]（長寛2年）に[[源義朝]]の家臣、大久保左馬之祐（さまのすけ）によって発見されたという<ref name="morinaga">[http://www.j-hss.org/journal/back_number/vol38_pdf/vol38no3_120_131.pdf 森永寛「放射能温泉地の医学－補遺」] 温泉科学38巻3号、2023年2月27日閲覧。</ref>。三朝温泉発祥の伝説として、白狼伝説があり、左馬之祐が[[三徳山]][[三仏寺]]の参拝に赴いた際、老いた白い[[オオカミ]]が現れ討ち取ろうとしたが、神仏の化身であることがわかると思いとどまり宿に帰った<ref name="misasa54730">[http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/files/54730.pdf みちくさウォーキングマップ 三朝・温泉街] 三朝町、2023年2月27日閲覧。</ref>。その夜、左馬之祐の夢枕に妙見大菩薩が現れて出湯の場所を道案内して消え、その教えられた場所に実際に行くと楠の老木の根元から湯が湧き出ていたという<ref name="horiuchi" /><ref name="misasa54730" />。三朝温泉源泉の「株湯」の名はこの故事に由来する<ref name="misasa54730" />。
温泉名の由来は諸説あり、その一つが「三つ目の朝を迎えるころには[[病気|病]]が消える」ことから三朝と呼ばれるようになったというものである<ref>【湯の心旅】三朝温泉（鳥取県）若旦那が変える湯治宿／医療と連携、森林セラピーも『[[日本経済新聞]]』朝刊2018年4月21日・別刷りNIKKEIプラス1（9面）</ref>。
『[[出雲国風土記]]』にも温泉に関する記述がある。明治以降、[[与謝野鉄幹]]、[[与謝野晶子]]、[[野口雨情]]、[[志賀直哉]]、[[斎藤茂吉]]、[[島崎藤村]]など多くの文人も訪れた。
[[1914年]]（大正3年）、三朝温泉の源泉の一つ（後の[[岡山大学]]分室泉）からラドンの存在が発見された<ref name="morinaga" />。[[1939年]]（昭和14年）に岡山医科大学が療養所を設置し、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）に放射能泉研究所に改組された<ref name="morinaga" />。この研究所は[[1949年]]（昭和24年）の新制岡山大学の発足により同大学附置の研究所となった<ref name="morinaga" />。
[[1947年]]（昭和22年）[[11月28日]]、昭和天皇が三朝温泉療養所に行幸（[[昭和天皇の戦後巡幸]]）<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=原武史 |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=99 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}</ref>。患者への慰問が行われた<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=宮内庁 |title=昭和天皇実録第十 |publisher=東京書籍 |year=2017-03-30 |page=548 |isbn=978-4-487-74410-7 }}</ref>。
==三朝温泉に関する作品== *[[三朝小唄]] *[[松本清張]]『[[Dの複合]]』 *[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[テレビドラマ]]『鳥帰る 故郷の母に会いたい』（[[田中好子]]主演、1996年5月4日放送）の[[ロケーション撮影|ロケ]]地 *映画『[[恋谷橋]]』（2011年公開）のロケ地 *テレビアニメ『[[宇崎ちゃんは遊びたい!]]』(2020年)、第10話で紹介された。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> 画像:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|河原風呂。よしずの向こうに脱衣場と足湯「河原の湯」がある 画像:Misasa onsen07n3648.jpg|温泉本通りの灯 画像:Misasa museum08st3200.jpg|三朝バイオリン美術館 画像:Jinsho-no-yakata02nt3200.jpg|陣所の館 File:Misasa onsen kabuyu 202106.jpg|公衆浴場「株湯」：右手前側には飲泉場がある File:Misasa jinja tyouzusha 202106.jpg|三朝神社の手水舎。温泉が流れており、飲泉できる。 </gallery>
== 周辺 == * [[三徳山]]（みとくさん）[[三佛寺|三仏寺]] - [[国宝]][[投入堂]]などが三朝温泉とともに2015年、[[文化庁]]から[[日本遺産]]「六根清浄と六感治癒の地」に認定されている<ref>[https://misasaonsen.jp/japan-heritage/ 日本遺産 三徳山 三朝温泉「六根清浄と六感治癒の地」]（2018年4月30日閲覧）</ref>。 * [[小鹿渓]] - 国の[[名勝]]。 * [[打吹玉川]] - [[重要伝統的建造物群保存地区]]。 * [[倉吉パークスクエア]] - [[倉吉未来中心]]、[[鳥取二十世紀梨記念館]]など。
== アクセス == * JR[[山陰本線]][[倉吉駅]]より、[[日ノ丸自動車|日ノ丸バス]]で20 - 25分。 * [[三朝温泉・はわい温泉直行かにバス]] * [[山陰自動車道]][[はわいインターチェンジ]]より[[国道179号]]・[[鳥取県道21号鳥取鹿野倉吉線]]経由。あるいは[[米子自動車道]][[湯原インターチェンジ]]より[[国道313号]]・[[国道482号]]・国道179号・鳥取県道21号線経由。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[ルブロバクテル・ラディオトレランス]] *[[鳥取県の観光地]] *[[美しい日本の歩きたくなるみち500選]] * [[リアリズムの宿]] - 2004年公開の映画のロケ地となった。 * [[ホルミシス効果]] * [[放射線ホルミシス#環境放射線の積極的な利用としての放射能泉]] * [[放射線ホルミシス#三朝温泉地区における調査]] * [[スーパーはくと#みささ|みささ (列車)]] - 旧国鉄が運行していた準急・急行列車
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ 三朝町] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ 三朝温泉公式サイト]
{{鳥取県の温泉}} {{温泉}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みささおんせん}} [[Category:鳥取県の温泉]] [[Category:三朝町の地理]] [[Category:地域団体商標]]

revision.text:
{{coord|35|24|38.7|N|133|53|35.1|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 三朝温泉 |画像 = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg |コメント = 河原風呂と三徳川 |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[東伯郡]][[三朝町]] |交通アクセス = 鉄道：JR[[山陰本線]][[倉吉駅]]より、[[日ノ丸自動車|日ノ丸バス]]で20 - 25分 |泉質 = [[塩化物泉]]、[[単純温泉]]、[[放射能泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=三朝温泉公式サイト |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher=鳥取県 輝く鳥取創造本部 観光交流局 観光戦略課 |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> |統計年度 = 2023 |外部リンク = [https://misasaonsen.jp/ 三朝温泉公式サイト] |特記事項 = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = 三朝温泉の位置 | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = 三朝温泉の位置 | alt = 三朝温泉の位置 }}
'''三朝温泉'''（みささ おんせん）は、[[鳥取県]][[東伯郡]][[三朝町]]にある[[温泉]]。[[日本百景]]。
2017年現在、年間入湯客数は鳥取県内では[[皆生温泉]]に次ぐ2位<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023" />。とっとり梨の花温泉郷を構成する温泉のひとつ。
== 泉質 == * 含放射能/ナトリウム・[[塩化物泉]] * 含放射能/単純泉
[[三徳川]]によって南北に三朝群（三朝区）と山田群（山田区）の二つの温泉群に分けられる<ref name="morinaga" /><ref name="horiuchi">[http://www.j-hss.org/journal/back_number/vol64_pdf/vol64no4_409_421.pdf 堀内公子、箕輪はるか、吉澤幸夫「三朝温泉におけるラドン研究の 100 年」] 温泉科学64巻、2023年2月27日閲覧。</ref>。日本最大規模の[[ラドン]]温泉地域で、泉温摂氏34度以上のラドン温泉は世界的にも希少な[[放射能泉]]である<ref name="horiuchi" />。
温泉療法を実施する医療機関があり、温泉は泉質に応じて温泉プール療法、[[飲泉]]療法、鉱泥療法など様々な治療に用いられている<ref>[http://www.okayama-u.ac.jp/user/misasa/spa/index.html 温泉療法 - 岡山大学病院三朝医療センター]（2012年7月21日閲覧）</ref><ref>[http://www.hosp.misasa.tottori.jp/about/onsen.html 当院の温泉について - 社団法人鳥取県中部医師会立三朝温泉病院]（2012年7月21日閲覧）</ref>以上により、高い[[ホルミシス効果]]があるとされ、[[観光客]]だけでなく療養目的で訪れる[[湯治]]客も多い<ref>{{Cite web |title=三朝温泉の効能と歴史 {{!}} 三朝温泉ポータルサイト│宿泊施設＆観光情報満載【三朝温泉 公式サイト】 |url=https://misasaonsen.jp/about/ |website=misasaonsen.jp |date=2020-03-20 |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=ラドン熱気浴 {{!}} 健康づくりの湯治宿 ブランナールみささ |url=https://www.blancart.jp/spa |work=健康づくりの湯治宿 ブランナールみささ |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref>。
== 温泉街 == 三徳川の両岸に[[旅館]]が立ち並ぶ<ref name="horiuchi" />。また、三朝大橋のたもとに河原風呂（川原の温泉：24時間（奇数日午前中は清掃）・無料）がある<ref>[https://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 三朝温泉河原風呂] 公益社団法人 鳥取県観光連盟、2023年3月15日閲覧。</ref>。[[石畳]]が敷かれた温泉本通りには、こぢんまりした旅館や飲食店、古美術店、スナック、[[土産物]]屋、[[射的]]場等が並んでおり、情緒ある[[温泉街]]が形成されている。三朝温泉最古の源泉とされる「株湯」は温泉街から少し離れた静かな住宅街の中にあり、地元住民の利用が多い<ref name="horiuchi" />。
本格的な療養温泉でもあり、[[温泉療法]]を実施する病院や研究施設がある。また温水床暖房を持った長期滞在者向けの旅館や自炊宿も見られ、[[観光]]と療養（[[湯治]]）の並立が三朝温泉の特徴である<ref name="horiuchi" />。二十数軒ある旅館・[[ホテル]]の多くが、「現代湯治」に対応している。温泉街にある病院での診察と組み合わせたり、[[ソムリエ]]をもじった入浴アドバイザー「ラヂムリエ」がいたりする旅館もある<ref>[http://spa-misasa.jp/toji/#toji_lodging 三朝温泉の現代湯治]（2018年4月30日閲覧）</ref>。
=== 温泉施設 === * [[外湯]] - 河原風呂、株湯 * [[足湯]] - 河原の湯、薬師の湯、かじかの湯、株湯 * [[飲泉]]場<ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=https://misasaonsen.jp/sightseeing/onsen | title=三朝温泉 温泉施設 | website=三朝温泉公式サイト | publisher=三朝温泉観光協会／三朝温泉旅館協同組合 | accessdate=2021年11月10日}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=https://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/176 | title=三朝温泉 | website=鳥取県観光案内 とっとり旅の生情報 | publisher=公益社団法人 鳥取県観光連盟 | accessdate=2021年11月10日}}</ref> - 薬師の湯、株湯、三朝神社（神の湯）
なお、三朝町営の[[国民宿舎]]として'''ブランナールみささ'''が設置された<ref>[http://www2.town.misasa.tottori.jp/reiki_int/reiki_honbun/m023RG00000378.html 三朝町国民宿舎事業の設置等に関する条例]</ref>。旧名称は三朝温泉会館で、[[1963年]]（昭和38年）にオープンし、[[1995年]]（平成7年）にブランナールみささに改名した。国民宿舎ブランナールみささは、[[2023年]]（令和5年）に指定管理者ジーライオンのグループ会社に譲渡されることになった<ref>[https://www.nnn.co.jp/articles/-/18978 三朝町「ブランナール」民間譲渡 指定管理者グループ会社に] 日本海新聞、2023年2月27日閲覧。</ref>。
また、温泉街には[[公衆浴場]]「たまわりの湯」が設けられ、住民や観光客に利用されてきたが、施設の老朽化と従業員の高齢化により、2023年3月末で営業を終了することになった<ref name="nnn20230314">[https://www.nnn.co.jp/articles/-/34336 ”癒やしの浴場”３月末閉館 三朝温泉・たまわりの湯 町が新施設計画も] 日本海新聞、2023年3月15日閲覧。</ref>。この共同浴場「たまわりの湯」の跡地についても、2024年（令和6年）5月に高級旅館の建設を計画するジーライオングループに売却する方針が発表された<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nnn.co.jp/articles/-/318771 |title=たまわりの湯跡地 売却へ 神戸の企業に 地元おおむね了承 三朝町|accessdate=2024-05-23 |publisher=日本海新聞 |language=ja}}</ref>。
=== 周辺施設 === * [[病院]] - 三朝温泉病院（2015年末で閉鎖の[[岡山大学病院三朝医療センター]]の機能も継承<ref>[https://mainichi.jp/articles/20151225/ddl/k31/040/506000c 三朝医療センター：岡大が今月末閉院 温泉病院が機能継承]『毎日新聞』朝刊2015年12月25日（鳥取県版）2018年4月30日閲覧</ref>） <!--療養型と思われるもの--> * 研究所 - [[岡山大学惑星物質研究所]]（岡山大学所管の共同利用・共同研究拠点） * [[博物館]]・[[美術館]] - [[三朝バイオリン美術館]]、[[陣所の館]] 、湯の街ギャラリー * その他 - [[キュリー広場]]、[[マリ・キュリー|キュリー夫人]]の胸像、[[木屋旅館]]（2010年3月 建物が国の[[登録有形文化財]]に登録）、[[旅館大橋]]（1932年築の国の[[登録有形文化財]]の旅館）
== 歴史 == 伝説によれば、[[平安時代]]末期の[[1164年]]（長寛2年）に[[源義朝]]の家臣、大久保左馬之祐（さまのすけ）によって発見されたという<ref name="morinaga">[http://www.j-hss.org/journal/back_number/vol38_pdf/vol38no3_120_131.pdf 森永寛「放射能温泉地の医学－補遺」] 温泉科学38巻3号、2023年2月27日閲覧。</ref>。三朝温泉発祥の伝説として、白狼伝説があり、左馬之祐が[[三徳山]][[三仏寺]]の参拝に赴いた際、老いた白い[[オオカミ]]が現れ討ち取ろうとしたが、神仏の化身であることがわかると思いとどまり宿に帰った<ref name="misasa54730">[http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/files/54730.pdf みちくさウォーキングマップ 三朝・温泉街] 三朝町、2023年2月27日閲覧。</ref>。その夜、左馬之祐の夢枕に妙見大菩薩が現れて出湯の場所を道案内して消え、その教えられた場所に実際に行くと楠の老木の根元から湯が湧き出ていたという<ref name="horiuchi" /><ref name="misasa54730" />。三朝温泉源泉の「株湯」の名はこの故事に由来する<ref name="misasa54730" />。
温泉名の由来は諸説あり、その一つが「三つ目の朝を迎えるころには[[病気|病]]が消える」ことから三朝と呼ばれるようになったというものである<ref>【湯の心旅】三朝温泉（鳥取県）若旦那が変える湯治宿／医療と連携、森林セラピーも『[[日本経済新聞]]』朝刊2018年4月21日・別刷りNIKKEIプラス1（9面）</ref>。
『[[出雲国風土記]]』にも温泉に関する記述がある。明治以降、[[与謝野鉄幹]]、[[与謝野晶子]]、[[野口雨情]]、[[志賀直哉]]、[[斎藤茂吉]]、[[島崎藤村]]など多くの文人も訪れた。
[[1914年]]（大正3年）、三朝温泉の源泉の一つ（後の[[岡山大学]]分室泉）からラドンの存在が発見された<ref name="morinaga" />。[[1939年]]（昭和14年）に岡山医科大学が療養所を設置し、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）に放射能泉研究所に改組された<ref name="morinaga" />。この研究所は[[1949年]]（昭和24年）の新制岡山大学の発足により同大学附置の研究所となった<ref name="morinaga" />。
[[1947年]]（昭和22年）[[11月28日]]、昭和天皇が三朝温泉療養所に行幸（[[昭和天皇の戦後巡幸]]）<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=原武史 |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=99 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}</ref>。患者への慰問が行われた<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=宮内庁 |title=昭和天皇実録第十 |publisher=東京書籍 |year=2017-03-30 |page=548 |isbn=978-4-487-74410-7 }}</ref>。
==三朝温泉に関する作品== *[[三朝小唄]] *[[松本清張]]『[[Dの複合]]』 *[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[テレビドラマ]]『鳥帰る 故郷の母に会いたい』（[[田中好子]]主演、1996年5月4日放送）の[[ロケーション撮影|ロケ]]地 *映画『[[恋谷橋]]』（2011年公開）のロケ地 *テレビアニメ『[[宇崎ちゃんは遊びたい!]]』(2020年)、第10話で紹介された。
== ギャラリー == <gallery> 画像:Misasa onsen02n4592.jpg|河原風呂。よしずの向こうに脱衣場と足湯「河原の湯」がある 画像:Misasa onsen07n3648.jpg|温泉本通りの灯 画像:Misasa museum08st3200.jpg|三朝バイオリン美術館 画像:Jinsho-no-yakata02nt3200.jpg|陣所の館 File:Misasa onsen kabuyu 202106.jpg|公衆浴場「株湯」：右手前側には飲泉場がある File:Misasa jinja tyouzusha 202106.jpg|三朝神社の手水舎。温泉が流れており、飲泉できる。 </gallery>
== 周辺 == * [[三徳山]]（みとくさん）[[三佛寺|三仏寺]] - [[国宝]][[投入堂]]などが三朝温泉とともに2015年、[[文化庁]]から[[日本遺産]]「六根清浄と六感治癒の地」に認定されている<ref>[https://misasaonsen.jp/japan-heritage/ 日本遺産 三徳山 三朝温泉「六根清浄と六感治癒の地」]（2018年4月30日閲覧）</ref>。 * [[小鹿渓]] - 国の[[名勝]]。 * [[打吹玉川]] - [[重要伝統的建造物群保存地区]]。 * [[倉吉パークスクエア]] - [[倉吉未来中心]]、[[鳥取二十世紀梨記念館]]など。
== アクセス == * JR[[山陰本線]][[倉吉駅]]より、[[日ノ丸自動車|日ノ丸バス]]で20 - 25分。 * [[三朝温泉・はわい温泉直行かにバス]] * [[山陰自動車道]][[はわいインターチェンジ]]より[[国道179号]]・[[鳥取県道21号鳥取鹿野倉吉線]]経由。あるいは[[米子自動車道]][[湯原インターチェンジ]]より[[国道313号]]・[[国道482号]]・国道179号・鳥取県道21号線経由。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[ルブロバクテル・ラディオトレランス]] *[[鳥取県の観光地]] *[[美しい日本の歩きたくなるみち500選]] * [[リアリズムの宿]] - 2004年公開の映画のロケ地となった。 * [[ホルミシス効果]] * [[放射線ホルミシス#環境放射線の積極的な利用としての放射能泉]] * [[放射線ホルミシス#三朝温泉地区における調査]] * [[スーパーはくと#みささ|みささ (列車)]] - 旧国鉄が運行していた準急・急行列車
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ 三朝町] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ 三朝温泉公式サイト]

{{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|qq=draft}} {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|qq=draft}} | Access = Rail: From JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|qq=draft}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = {{ill|Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|qq=draft}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|qq=draft}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景|qq=draft}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers Ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉|qq=draft}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷|qq=draft}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|qq=draft}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|qq=draft}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉|qq=draft}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|qq=draft}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|qq=draft}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|qq=draft}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|qq=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|qq=draft}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|qq=draft}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|qq=draft}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|qq=draft}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|qq=draft}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|qq=draft}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|qq=draft}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|qq=draft}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|qq=draft}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21. == References == {{Footnote Help}} <references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂|qq=draft}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]]

In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|qq=draft}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|qq=draft}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|qq=draft}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|qq=draft}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|qq=draft}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|qq=draft}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|qq=draft}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|qq=draft}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|qq=draft}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|qq=draft}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21. == References ==

{{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi Station|qq=}}, take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chloride Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}}, {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher={{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Prefecture|qq=}} |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōhaku District|ja|東伯郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhaku District|qq=}}, {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Town|qq=}}. It is recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's 100 Views|ja|日本百景|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's 100 Views|qq=}}.
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers Ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike Onsen|qq=}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|qq=}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive spring|qq=}} / Sodium {{ill|brine spring|lt=Chloride Hot Spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/brine spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Brine spring|qq=}} * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive spring|qq=}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|qq=}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the {{ill|Sanyō region|ja|山陽地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyō region|qq=}}, this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as {{ill|Hagi City|ja|萩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hagi City|qq=}} and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuwano|qq=}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen|qq=}}, Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|qq=}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|qq=}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|qq=}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefectural University|qq=}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Shrine|qq=}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santoku Mountain|qq=}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbutsu-ji|qq=}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōnyūdō|qq=}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikakan Kei|qq=}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchibuki Tamagawa|qq=}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi Park Square|qq=}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi Station|qq=}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Main Line|qq=}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinonmaru Bus|qq=}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyō Expressway|qq=}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonago Expressway|qq=}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21. == References ==

== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|qq=}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]]

The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|qq=}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|qq=}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|qq=}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefectural University|ja|山口県立大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefectural University|qq=}} have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Shrine|qq=}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * {{ill|Santoku Mountain|ja|三徳山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Santoku Mountain|qq=}} (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbutsu-ji|qq=}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōnyūdō|qq=}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikakan Kei|qq=}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchibuki Tamagawa|qq=}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi Park Square|qq=}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|ja|倉吉駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi Station|qq=}} on the JR {{ill|San'in Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Main Line|qq=}} via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinonmaru Bus|qq=}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the {{ill|Sanyō Expressway|ja|山陰自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyō Expressway|qq=}} from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the {{ill|Yonago Expressway|ja|米子自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonago Expressway|qq=}} via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21. == References ==

{{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]] [[:en:Misasa, Tottori|Misasa Town]] | Access = Rail: From JR [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] at [[:en:Kurayoshi Station|Kurayoshi Station]], take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinonmaru Bus|qq=}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]], {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher=[[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]], [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]], [[:en:Misasa, Tottori|Misasa Town]]. It is recognized as one of [[:en:100 Landscapes of Japan (Shōwa era)|Japan's 100 Views]].
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers Ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike Onsen|qq=}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|qq=}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive spring|qq=}} / Sodium [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride Hot Spring]] * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive spring|qq=}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|qq=}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the [[:en:San'yō region|Sanyō region]], this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as [[:en:Hagi|Hagi City]] and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuwano|qq=}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|qq=}}, and [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]], Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|qq=}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|qq=}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|qq=}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefectural University|Yamaguchi Prefectural University]] have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Shrine|qq=}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * [[:en:Mount Mitoku|Santoku Mountain]] (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbutsu-ji|qq=}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōnyūdō|qq=}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikakan Kei|qq=}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchibuki Tamagawa|qq=}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi Park Square|qq=}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from [[:en:Kurayoshi Station|Kurayoshi Station]] on the JR [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinonmaru Bus|qq=}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the [[:en:San'in Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]] from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the [[:en:Yonago Expressway|Yonago Expressway]] via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21. == References ==

Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * [[:en:Mount Mitoku|Santoku Mountain]] (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbutsu-ji|qq=}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōnyūdō|qq=}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikakan Kei|qq=}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchibuki Tamagawa|qq=}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi Park Square|qq=}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from [[:en:Kurayoshi Station|Kurayoshi Station]] on the JR [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinonmaru Bus|qq=}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the [[:en:San'in Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]] from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the [[:en:Yonago Expressway|Yonago Expressway]] via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21. == References ==
<references />

revision.text:
{{Source Citation|date=May 2015}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Misasa Onsen (三朝温泉) | image = Misasa onsen kawaranoyu 202106.jpg | Comment = Kawahara Bath and Santokugawa | Location = [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]] [[:en:Misasa, Tottori|Misasa Town]] | Access = Rail: From JR [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] at [[:en:Kurayoshi Station|Kurayoshi Station]], take {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinonmaru Bus|qq=}} for 20–25 minutes | Spring Quality = [[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]], {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}}, {{ill|Radioactive Hot Spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive Hot Spring|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = 22<ref>{{Cite web |title=宿泊施設|url=https://misasaonsen.jp/stay/ |website=Misasa Onsen Official Site |access-date=2024-10-04 |language=ja}}</ref> | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = 289,719<ref name="PREF-TT-chousa2023">{{Cite web |url=https://www.pref.tottori.lg.jp/70595.htm |title=観光入込動態調査 温泉地入湯客数 |access-date=2024-10-04 |publisher=[[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]] |date=2024-09-13}}</ref> | Statistical Year = 2023 | Special Notes = }} {{Location map | Japan Tottori | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 24 | lat_sec = 38.7 | lat_dir = N | lon_deg = 133 | lon_min = 53 | lon_sec = 35.1 | lon_dir = E | label = Location of Misasa Onsen | position = bottom | mark = | marksize = 6 | width = 350 | float = right | caption = Location of Misasa Onsen | alt = Location of Misasa Onsen }}
'''Misasa Onsen''' (三朝温泉) is a hot spring located in [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]], [[:en:Tōhaku District|Tōhaku District]], [[:en:Misasa, Tottori|Misasa Town]]. It is recognized as one of [[:en:100 Landscapes of Japan (Shōwa era)|Japan's 100 Views]].
As of 2017, the annual number of bathers Ranks second in Tottori Prefecture, following {{ill|Kaike Onsen|ja|皆生温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaike Onsen|qq=}}. It is one of the hot springs that constitute the {{ill|Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|ja|とっとり梨の花温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Nashinohana Onsen Resort|qq=}}.
== Spring Quality == * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive spring|qq=}} / Sodium [[:en:Brine spring|Chloride Hot Spring]] * {{ill|Radioactive hot spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|1=Radioactive spring|lt=Radon-containing|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive spring|qq=}} / {{ill|Simple Hot Springs|lt=Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} They are Japan’s largest radon hot spring region, and radon hot springs with temperatures above 34°C are extremely rare worldwide.
Medical institutions implement hot spring therapy using these springs, which are utilized in treatments such as hot spring pool therapy, hot spring drinking therapy, and mineral mud therapy. Consequently, the hot springs are believed to exhibit significant hormesis effects, attracting therapeutic bathers as well as tourists.
== Onsen Town == [[File:Hotspring Yuda Yamaguchi 01.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A foot bath within {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|qq=}} (formerly Takada Park)]] The onsen town, located in the southwestern part of Yamaguchi City, features numerous accommodations including large hotels, traditional ryokan, and business hotels. Although few onsen towns exist along the [[:en:San'yō region|Sanyō region]], this is the premier onsen town on the Sanyō route. It also serves as a major lodging base for tourists visiting Yamaguchi City as well as for those heading to renowned sightseeing destinations such as [[:en:Hagi|Hagi City]] and {{ill|Tsuwano|ja|津和野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuwano|qq=}}, supported by an extensive bus network.
The area also functions as the central commercial and entertainment district of Yamaguchi City, offering many restaurants. Unlike onsen that emerge in the urban centers of prefectural capitals such as {{ill|Kōfu Onsen|ja|甲府温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori Onsen|ja|鳥取温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|ja|松江しんじ湖温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue Shinji Lake Onsen|qq=}}, and [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]], Misasa Onsen is located within a densely built urban area. Although it lacks the traditional atmosphere of a historic onsen town, several spots exist for nighttime strolls.
The Matsudaya Hotel in the onsen town is reputed as a lodging where patriots of the late Edo period once gathered. In addition, the hotel was selected by critic {{ill|Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|ja|石井宏子 (評論家)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Hiroko (Critic)|qq=}} as one of “10 Inns to Savor Gourmet Cuisine” and was also listed among “200 Recommended Inns & Communal Baths” by {{ill|Matsuda Tadanori|ja|松田忠徳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuda Tadanori|qq=}}.
The town places strong emphasis on its foot baths, with five facilities including {{ill|Inoue Park|ja|井上公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Park|qq=}} (formerly Takada Park). Since October 17, 2005, the Misasa Onsen Distribution Cooperative and [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefectural University|Yamaguchi Prefectural University]] have jointly conducted efficacy tests on these foot baths. In March 2015, Yamaguchi City opened a paid tourist circulation facility called “Fox’s Footprints.”
== Onsen Facilities == * Public Baths – Kawahara-yu (河原の湯) and Kabayu (株湯) * Foot Baths – Kawahara no Yu (河原の湯), Yakushi no Yu (薬師の湯), Kajika no Yu (かじかの湯), Kabayu (株湯) * Hot Spring Drinking Facilities – Yakushi no Yu, Kabayu, and the “Kami-no-Yu” at {{ill|Misasa Shrine|ja|三朝神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Shrine|qq=}} In addition, as a municipally operated national lodging facility in Misasa Town, “Brannal Misasa” was established. Its former name was Misasa Onsen Kaikan, which opened in 1963 (Shōwa 38) and was renamed Brannal Misasa in 1995 (Heisei 7). Brannal Misasa was transferred in 2023 (Reiwa 5) to a group company of G-Lion, the designated administrator. Furthermore, there was a communal bath called “Tamahari no Yu” that served both residents and visitors; however, due to aging facilities and an aging workforce, it ceased operations at the end of March 2023. The site of “Tamahari no Yu” is planned to be sold in May 2024 (Reiwa 6) to the G-Lion Group, which intends to construct a high-end ryokan there.
== Surrounding Areas == * [[:en:Mount Mitoku|Santoku Mountain]] (Mitoku-san) and {{ill|Sanbutsu-ji|ja|三仏寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbutsu-ji|qq=}} – Along with the Nikkō Heritage Site “Land of Purification of the Six Roots and Healing of the Six Senses” (六根清浄と六感治癒の地) designated by the Agency for Cultural Affairs in 2015, featuring the {{ill|Tōnyūdō|ja|投入堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōnyūdō|qq=}} among others. * {{ill|Shikakan Kei|ja|小鹿渓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikakan Kei|qq=}} – A National Scenic Area. * {{ill|Uchibuki Tamagawa|ja|打吹玉川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchibuki Tamagawa|qq=}} – An Important Traditional Buildings Preservation District. * {{ill|Kurayoshi Park Square|ja|倉吉パークスクエア|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurayoshi Park Square|qq=}} – Including the Kurayoshi Future Center and the Tottori 20th Century Pear Memorial Museum.
== Access == * Rail: Approximately 20–25 minutes by bus from [[:en:Kurayoshi Station|Kurayoshi Station]] on the JR [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] via {{ill|Hinonmaru Bus|ja|日ノ丸自動車|lt=Hinonmaru Bus|lt_ja=日ノ丸バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinonmaru Bus|qq=}}. * Bus: Direct bus service for Misasa Onsen / Hawai Onsen. * By Car: Accessible via the [[:en:San'in Expressway|Sanyō Expressway]] from the Hawai Interchange via National Route 179 and Tottori Prefecture Road 21 (Tottori-Kanago-Kurayoshi Line); alternatively, from the [[:en:Yonago Expressway|Yonago Expressway]] via National Routes 313 and 482, National Route 179, and Tottori Prefecture Road 21. == References ==
<references />
== External Links == {{commonscat|Misasa Onsen}} * [http://www.tottori-guide.jp/tourism/tour/view/178 {{ill|Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|ja|三朝温泉河原風呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misasa Onsen Public Bath – Kawahara no Yu|qq=}}] – Public Information, Tottori Prefecture Tourism Federation, March 15, 2023. * [http://www.town.misasa.tottori.jp/japan/ {{ill|Misasa Town|ja|三朝町}]] * [https://misasaonsen.jp/ {{ill|Misasa Onsen Official Site|ja|三朝温泉公式サイト}]]
